items.title: Draft REP 158: OpenUSD Conventions for Simulation Asset Interoperability in Open Source Robotics
items.summary:
Dear ROS Community,

Following the REP-0001:2025 process, I am opening this public discussion thread for Draft REP 158: OpenUSD Conventions for Simulation Asset Interoperability in Open Source Robotics.

The goal of this REP is to define a standardized way to use OpenUSD (Universal Scene Description)

items.title: [Release] AI Flight Integrity Observer: Measuring PX4 Offboard degradation under controlled AI inference lag
items.summary:
Hi guys,

I’d like to share AI Flight Integrity Observer (AFIO), an open-source ROS 2 / PX4 runtime assurance observer for Offboard autonomy.

AFIO is designed to make one failure surface visible:

items.title: Fast DDS v3.4.3
items.summary: Fast DDS v3.4.3 (EOL) This is the last release for the branch 3.4.x , since it has reached EOL. This patch release includes the following security fixes : CVE-2026-22591 CVE-2026-45094 CVE-2026-45095 CVE-2026-45096 CVE-2025-63829 CVE-2025-65865 CVE-2026-45093 CVE-2026-45092 CVE-2026-45097 It also includes the following fixes : Fix RTPSParticipantAttributes internal data races ( #6387 ) Fix some null-dereference errors ( #6397 , #6405 ) Fix wrong update of default locators ( #6279 ) Make on_xxx_m

items.title: A near-autonomous AI chemist improves a challenging reaction in medicinal chemistry
items.summary: OpenAI and Molecule.one show how a near-autonomous AI chemist using GPT-5.4 improved a key drug-making reaction, advancing medicinal chemistry research.

items.title: Introducing LifeSciBench
items.summary: Introducing LifeSciBench, an expert-authored, expert-reviewed benchmark for evaluating how AI systems handle real-world life science research tasks and decisions.

items.title: LiteLLM v1.88.3
items.summary: Verify Docker Image Signature All LiteLLM Docker images are signed with cosign . Every release is signed with the same key introduced in commit 0112e53 . Verify using the pinned commit hash (recommended): A commit hash is cryptographically immutable, so this is the strongest way to ensure you are using the original signing key: cosign verify \ --key https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/0112e53046018d726492c814b3644b7d376029d0/cosign.pub \ ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:v1.88.3 Verify using
